input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to know how to migrate their configuration to the modern MSTest format. Let me look at their files to understand their current setup.

data.content:
## Migration: `.testsettings` → `.runsettings`

The `.testsettings` format is **deprecated** and ignored by modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Global test timeout (30 seconds) -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <!-- Per-test method timeout -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Global test timeout (30 seconds) -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <!-- Per-test method timeout -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
### What changed

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| Deployment / AssemblyResolution | `<DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>` |
| CodeCoverage DataCollector | Moved under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` with updated URI |

### How to use it

Either pass it explicitly:

```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```

Or wire it into your `.csproj` so it's automatic:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
</PropertyGroup>
```

You can now safely delete `local.testsettings` — it has no effect with MSTest v3.
